summary: Round-2 verification against code: Theme.SanctumMachina → Theme.Sanctum fix confirmed (zero residual references; themes.xml declares only Theme.Sanctum). The architecture.md § Testing anchor was fixed at line 246 (now cites code-research §9.1 correctly) BUT the same stale anchor still appears in Task 10 description at line 385 — partial fix, one critical-path instruction to `test-master` still points at a non-existent doc heading. The new round-2 additions contain one critical mirage: CrashHandlerTest prescribes `InOrder` for call-order assertions, which implies Mockito or MockK — neither library is on the classpath (no mockito/mockk in libs.versions.toml, no testImplementation in app/build.gradle.kts) and code-research §9.3 explicitly states 'no MockK dependency; tests use hand-rolled fakes'. Implementor will either introduce an unapproved dependency or be blocked. All other round-2 API claims verify: Application.getProcessName() is API 28+ (safe at minSdk 31), ProcessBuilder.redirectErrorStream / Process.destroy are standard JDK, Process.waitFor(long, TimeUnit) is API 26+. The ShadowContentResolver IOException claim is plausible (Robolectric's ShadowContentResolver.registerOutputStream lets tests substitute an OutputStream whose write() throws) and flagged as minor — wording should be tightened because 'force IOException from openOutputStream' overstates the shadow's API (openOutputStream returns whatever was registered; throwing happens on write, not on open).

findings.claim: tech-spec.md §Testing Strategy line 237: 'CrashHandlerTest — uncaught exception writes crash.log before the injected Killer is invoked (assert call order via InOrder)'. `InOrder` is the Mockito API for verifying invocation order across multiple mocks.
findings.reality: Mockito is NOT declared anywhere in gradle/libs.versions.toml or app/build.gradle.kts. MockK is also absent. code-research.md §9.3 line 290 explicitly states: 'Mocks via hand-rolled fakes (no MockK dependency in libs.versions.toml)'. ChatViewModelTest, ErrorLogTest, SafeUriHandlerTest — the three reference Kotlin test files — import only org.junit.*, kotlinx.coroutines.test.*, androidx.test.core.*, org.robolectric.*. Zero uses of InOrder in the codebase. Using `InOrder` in CrashHandlerTest would either force a new dependency (policy-breaking: tech-spec.md §Dependencies says 'None' for new packages and Acceptance Criteria bullet 10 demands 'No new external dependencies in gradle/libs.versions.toml or app/build.gradle.kts') or the test won't compile.
findings.fix: Replace 'assert call order via InOrder' with the project-idiomatic pattern: extend the hand-rolled Killer fake to record a monotonically-incrementing order index and the filesystem state visible at the moment of the kill call, then assert `killer.orderIndex > 0 && killer.filesystemSnapshot.crashLogExisted`. Example shape already used in ChatViewModelTest fakes. Alternatively: a `recordedEvents: MutableList<String>` that both the filesystem-write-observer and the Killer append to, with `assertEquals(listOf("wrote-crash-log", "killed"), events)`.

findings.claim: tech-spec.md §Implementation Tasks > Task 10 (line 385): 'pyramid balance (size M → unit-only is correct; integration/E2E absence justified by architecture.md § Testing)'.
findings.reality: `architecture.md` has no `## Testing` or `### Testing` heading — only inline bolded 'Testing:' paragraphs at lines 51 and 262. The round-1 skeptic review flagged this exact anchor as major-severity and the fix was applied at tech-spec line 246 (now correctly cites code-research §9.1). Task 10 at line 385 was missed in the fix sweep and still uses the stale anchor. `test-master` running Task 10 will attempt to open architecture.md § Testing and either find nothing applicable or the 'JUnit 4.13.2 + Robolectric' one-liner which does not substantiate 'integration/E2E absence'.
findings.fix: Replace `architecture.md § Testing` with `code-research §9.1` at line 385 (matching the fix already applied at line 246). Alternatively reference the concrete rationale directly: 'integration/E2E absence justified by the absence of androidTest sourceSet and Compose-ui-test classpath — see code-research §9.1'.

findings.claim: tech-spec.md §Testing Strategy (line 248): 'LogExportManagerTest.writeTo_ioException_surfaces stubs ContentResolver via Robolectric's ShadowContentResolver to force an IOException on the write path'.
findings.reality: Robolectric's ShadowContentResolver (org.robolectric.shadows.ShadowContentResolver) exposes `registerOutputStream(Uri, OutputStream)` and `registerInputStream(Uri, InputStream)`. It does NOT expose a dedicated 'forceIOException' method. The test achieves the effect indirectly: it registers an OutputStream whose `write(ByteArray)` / `close()` throws IOException (a hand-rolled `object : OutputStream() { override fun write(b: Int) = throw IOException() }`). The distinction matters because the IOException fires on the first write call, not on `openOutputStream` itself — so the spec's phrasing on line 30 ('a null return is treated as IOException("openOutputStream returned null")') and the test description on line 248 describe two different failure paths. Both are legitimate, but the spec conflates them into one test name.
findings.fix: Split the test coverage explicitly: (a) `writeTo_openOutputStreamReturnsNull_throwsIOException` — ShadowContentResolver with no registered stream (returns null), asserts the explicit throw of `IOException("openOutputStream returned null")`; (b) `writeTo_writeCallThrows_surfacesIOException` — ShadowContentResolver with a registered OutputStream that throws on write(). Two test cases, two line items in §Testing Strategy. Keeps the caller's single-error-path contract honest.

findings.claim: tech-spec.md §Dependencies line 225 lists test infra as 'org.robolectric:robolectric 4.12, androidx.test:core 1.6.1, kotlinx-coroutines-test 1.10.2, junit:junit 4.13.2'.
findings.reality: All four versions verify against libs.versions.toml (junit=4.13.2 line 18, robolectric=4.12 line 19, coroutinesTest=1.10.2 line 23, androidxTestCore=1.6.1 line 24). However, .claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/architecture.md line 51 states 'MockK is available' — MockK is NOT on the classpath (confirmed via grep across gradle/libs.versions.toml and app/build.gradle.kts). This is a project-knowledge drift, not a tech-spec issue, but tech-spec inherits the risk indirectly by telling new tests to 'mirror the Robolectric pattern of ErrorLogTest.kt' — which is correct, while architecture.md is wrong.
findings.fix: No tech-spec change required. Independent issue: fix architecture.md line 51 to say 'MockK is NOT on the classpath; tests use hand-rolled fakes or FakeDataStore + TemporaryFolder.' Noted again because the round-1 report flagged the patterns.md variant of this drift; project-knowledge repair is still pending.
